counterfactual: Column-only qty/price extraction; no multiplier inference from description; no invented rows; lot/sub-line rejection

results.rationale: Column visibly shows qty=2. Removing infer-from-name (L33-34) and pack-*N examples (L53-84) eliminates *6→6 override. Fresh extract already shows qty=2 once postfix geometry fixed.

Price error persists in fresh extract (qty=2 correct, price still €20). Column-only rule helps intent but vision digit misread (25→20) may survive as model/OCR limitation independent of pack inference.
Total follows qty and price. Fixing qty (6→2) fixes €120→€50. Remaining €40 vs €50 depends on price counterfactual (UNCERTAIN).
Column shows qty=1. No multiplier inference eliminates *2→2. Fresh extract (2026-06-11) already corrected to qty=1 with current prompt — proves column value is readable when inference suppressed.
Same mechanism as Aceto. Fresh extract corrected to qty=1. Column-only extraction resolves.
No invented rows + lot/sub-line rejection + row-count guard (8 artigo codes) eliminates 9th row. Geometry shows exactly 8 real rows; phantom is pure GPT invention.
Column shift / digit bleed from weight range '4-4,25KG'. Column-only mandate reduces contextual bleed but dense Italian grid misalignment is primarily vision limitation.
Decimal truncation suggests column misread not pack inference. Column-only helps but fractional qty parsing may still fail.
Wrong price column read. Not caused by pack inference; column isolation guidance may help but not guaranteed.
Direct application of pack-24-bottles example (L53-55) and bad-good-33cl (L80-84). Column shows qty=2 cases. No multiplier inference fixes.

results.error: Emporio Ginger Beer unit 2 un vs 24 un
results.rationale: Follows qty error. Column-only extraction yields correct case/unit semantics.
Per-bottle price substituted for per-case price as consequence of qty=24 inference. Fixing qty restores case-level pricing.

summary.visionResidualErrors: 4/12 (33%) — column shift / digit misread independent of pack inference
